summary: High‑energy 1983 show featuring the first St. Stephen in years and a rare Bertha > China Doll, captured on an acceptable recording that fans recommend as matrix or audience.
review: The concert is celebrated for its electric vibe – debuting St. Stephen after a five‑year hiatus, breakout Wang Dang Doodle, and a memorable Bertha > China Doll pairing – with the second set building even more momentum. Reviewers note ragged vocal moments but feel the stage energy outweighs any imperfections, and they suggest listening to a matrix or audience mix for the best experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers praise the performance highly; recording quality is not criticized but only vaguely referenced, leading to a strong overall rating with moderate confidence due to the small number of reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit comments on clarity or mix; reviewers advise using a matrix or audience source, implying the existing FLAC is at least listenable but not definitively characterized.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set charged with high energy and several first‑time performances; second set steps up further, especially the pre‑drums run highlighted by a powerful Bertha.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil sings on China Doll (using his own mic), noted as a rare occurrence at the time.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums run is highlighted as especially rocking, indicating strong drum buildup.
